info.name: Advertiser Chat
info.description:
Advertiser chat — one chat per advertiser, used to contact admins for support.

**Sending messages is done via Socket.IO ONLY** (see CHAT-SOCKET.md). The REST endpoints here are only for:
1. Logging in (to get the JWT used by the socket).
2. Loading the chat and its message history when opening the chat screen.

Base URL: `baseUrl` variable (default `http://localhost:3005/api`).
Socket URL: `ws://localhost:3005/chat`.

item.name: Login (Advertiser)

exec: const token = res.accessToken || res.access_token || (res.data && (res.data.accessToken || res.data.access_token)) || res.token;
console.log('Saved accessToken to collection variables. Use this same token in the Socket.IO auth.token field.');
console.warn('No access token found in login response. Inspect the response body and set {{accessToken}} manually.');

item.description:
Logs the advertiser in and saves the JWT to `{{accessToken}}`.

Use this same token for the socket connection: `io('http://localhost:3005/chat', { auth: { token: '<accessToken>' } })`.

request.description:
Returns the advertiser's chat (creates it on first call) together with a paginated list of its messages.

Each advertiser has exactly ONE chat with the shared admin pool.

**Query params:**
- `page` (default 1)
- `limit` (default 10)

**Response:**
```
{
  chat: { id, advertiserId, lastMessageAt, createdAt, updatedAt },
  messages: {
    data: ChatMessage[],   // newest first
    pagination: { total, page, limit }
  }
}
```

`ChatMessage` shape:
```
{
  id, chatId, senderId,
  senderRole: 'advertiser' | 'admin',
  content,
  attachmentUrl, attachmentPublicId,
  createdAt
}
```

Use this when the user opens the chat screen — render the messages, THEN open the socket to receive new ones in real time.
